aggregateOn.description: Field name to aggregate on (e.g., 'response_time', 'duration'). Required for all aggregations except count and rate.

aggregation.description: Aggregation function to apply. One of: count, count_distinct, avg, sum, min, max, p50, p75, p90, p95, p99, rate

end.description: End time in unix milliseconds (optional). When both start and end are provided, they override timeRange.

filter.description: Filter expression using SigNoz search syntax (see signoz://logs/query-builder-guide). Combine conditions with AND, OR, and parentheses for precedence. Unknown keys hard-error; keys present in multiple contexts default to resource context. Disambiguate with attribute.<key> or resource.<key>. Discover valid keys with signoz_get_field_keys, then confirm values with signoz_get_field_values, before filtering. Examples: "service.name = 'payment-svc' AND severity_text = 'ERROR'", "(severity_text = 'ERROR' OR body CONTAINS 'panic') AND k8s.namespace.name = 'prod'", "body.user.id = '123'". Combined with service/severity params using AND.

groupBy.description: Comma-separated list of field names to group results by (e.g., 'service.name' or 'service.name, severity_text'). Leave empty for a single aggregate value.

limit.description: Maximum number of groups to return (default: 10, max: 10000; higher values are clamped)

orderBy.description: How to order results. Format: '<expression> <direction>', e.g. 'count() desc' or 'avg(duration) asc'. Defaults to the aggregation expression descending.

requestType.description:
Controls whether to return a single aggregate or a time-series. Choose based on the user's question — do NOT ask the user to set this.

"scalar" (default) — Returns one aggregate value computed over the entire time range. Use when the answer is a single number or a ranked/grouped table: "how many errors today?", "what is the p99 latency of checkout?", "which service has the most errors?", "top 10 slowest endpoints".

"time_series" — Returns one value per time bucket so you can see changes over time. Use ONLY when the user's question is about WHEN something happened, HOW a metric changed, or to find SPIKES/TRENDS across time: "when did errors spike?", "how did p99 change hour by hour?", "show error count per hour", "at what time is traffic highest?".

If the intent is ambiguous (e.g. "show latency over 24h" could mean either), ask the user to clarify before calling this tool.

IMPORTANT: If the question has ANY temporal component (spike, trend, change over time, "when did X happen"), always use "time_series" — it answers both the count AND the timing in one call. Never call this tool twice for the same question.
Example: "get error count and find when it spiked" → "time_series".

tools.name: signoz_create_alert

alert.description: Name of the alert rule. Must be unique and descriptive.

alertType.description: Signal type: METRIC_BASED_ALERT or LOGS_BASED_ALERT or TRACES_BASED_ALERT or EXCEPTIONS_BASED_ALERT.

annotations.description: Annotations like description and summary. Supports template variables: {{$value}} for current metric value and {{$threshold}} for the threshold and {{$labels.key}} for label values.

condition.description: Alert condition containing the query and threshold configuration.

absentFor.description: Minutes (equivalent to consecutive evaluation cycles when frequency is 1m) to wait with no data before firing an absent-data alert. Example: absentFor=15 with frequency=1m fires after 15 evaluations return no series.

alertOnAbsent.description: Alert when no data is received within the evaluation window.

algorithm.description: v1 (anomaly_rule) only. Anomaly detection algorithm. Accepted values include standard (z-score based). Used only when ruleType=anomaly_rule.

compositeQuery.description: The composite query defining what data to monitor.

panelType.description: Panel type. Use graph for alerts. Defaults to graph.

queries.description: Array of queries. At least one query is required.

aggregations.description: Aggregation expressions for builder queries. For metrics signal use the object shape: [{metricName: k8s.pod.cpu_request_utilization, timeAggregation: avg, spaceAggregation: max}]. For logs/traces use the expression shape: [{expression: count()}] or [{expression: p99(duration_nano)}].

expression.description: Aggregation expression (logs/traces signal). Examples: count(), avg(duration), p99(duration_nano), count_distinct(user_id), sum(bytes). Do not set metricName/timeAggregation/spaceAggregation when using this shape.

metricName.description: Metric name (metrics signal only). Example: k8s.pod.cpu_request_utilization. Use alongside timeAggregation and spaceAggregation. Do not set expression when using this shape.

spaceAggregation.description: Cross-series space aggregation (metrics signal only). Common values: sum, avg, min, max, count. For histograms use percentiles: p50, p75, p90, p95, p99.

timeAggregation.description: Per-series time aggregation (metrics signal only). Common values: avg, max, min, sum, rate, increase, count, count_distinct, latest. Default by metric type: gauge→avg, cumulative counter→rate, delta counter→sum.

expression.description: Formula expression referencing other query names (e.g. A / B * 100). Used for builder_formula type.

filter.description: Filter expression for builder queries. Example: {expression: service.name = frontend AND http.status_code >= 500}.

expression.description: Filter expression using field operators. Example: service.name = frontend AND http.status_code >= 500. Use empty string for no filter.

functions.description: Post-query functions applied to the series. Required for anomaly_rule: wrap with {name: anomaly, args: [{name: z_score_threshold, value: 2}]}.

args.description: Function arguments. Example for anomaly: [{name: z_score_threshold, value: 2}].

name.description: Argument name (e.g. z_score_threshold).

value.description: Argument value. Can be number, string, or bool depending on the function.

name.description: Function name (e.g. anomaly for ruleType=anomaly_rule).

groupBy.description: Fields to group by. Grouped dimensions appear as labels in alert notifications.

fieldContext.description: Field context: resource for resource attributes or tag for span/log attributes. Required for non-top-level fields.

fieldDataType.description: Data type of the field: string or int64 or float64 or bool.

name.description: Field name to group by (e.g. service.name or http.method or severity_text).

having.description: Having clause to filter aggregation results.

expression.description: Filter expression using field operators. Example: service.name = frontend AND http.status_code >= 500. Use empty string for no filter.

name.description: Query name (e.g. A or B or C). Used as reference in formulas and selectedQueryName.
Field or aggregation expression to order by (e.g. timestamp or count()).

query.description: PromQL or ClickHouse SQL query string. Used when queryType is promql or clickhouse_sql. PromQL with OTel dotted metric names MUST use the Prometheus 3.x UTF-8 quoted-selector form: {"metric.name.with.dots"}. Underscored / __name__ / bare-dotted forms return no data. Read signoz://promql/instructions for the full guide (histogram patterns dotted labels pre-flight checklist).

signal.description: Signal type for builder queries: metrics or logs or traces. Required for builder_query type.

source.description: Data-source filter for metrics builder_query only. Set to meter to alert on Cost Meter (usage/billing) metrics such as signoz.meter.log.size; omit otherwise.

stepInterval.description: Step interval in seconds for time aggregation. Use 60 for metrics alerts.

type.description: Query envelope type. Must match compositeQuery.queryType: builder → builder_query or builder_formula; promql → promql; clickhouse_sql → clickhouse_sql. Also accepted: builder_trace_operator for trace operator queries.

queryType.description: Query type: builder for Query Builder or promql for PromQL or clickhouse_sql for ClickHouse SQL.

unit.description: Unit of the queried data (Y-axis unit). Used for value formatting in alert messages and for unit conversion with targetUnit in thresholds. Common values: percent, ms, s, bytes, ns, reqps, ops.

matchType.description: v1 (anomaly_rule) only. Match type - same accepted values as threshold.matchType (at_least_once, all_the_times, on_average/avg, in_total/sum, last).

op.description: v1 (anomaly_rule) only. Comparison operator applied to the anomaly score - same accepted values as threshold.op (above, below, equal, not_equal, above_or_equal, below_or_equal, outside_bounds).

requireMinPoints.description: Require a minimum number of data points before evaluating the condition.

requiredNumPoints.description: Minimum number of data points required when requireMinPoints is true.

seasonality.description: v1 (anomaly_rule) only. Seasonality pattern for anomaly detection: hourly, daily, or weekly.

selectedQueryName.description: Which query name triggers the alert (e.g. A or B or F1). Required when multiple queries exist. Defaults to the first query name.

target.description: v1 (anomaly_rule) only. Threshold value compared against the anomaly score.

thresholds.description: v2alpha1 only (threshold_rule, promql_rule). Each threshold level (critical, error, warning, info) can route to different notification channels. Required unless alertOnAbsent is true. Omit entirely for anomaly_rule - use condition.op/matchType/target there instead.

kind.description: Threshold kind. Currently only basic is supported.

spec.description: Array of threshold specifications. Each threshold can route to different channels.

channels.description: Notification channel names for this threshold tier. Use signoz_list_notification_channels to discover available names. Ignored when notificationSettings.usePolicy is true.

matchType.description: How to evaluate the threshold. Canonical: at_least_once, all_the_times, on_average, in_total, last. Aliases accepted: avg (=on_average), sum (=in_total). Numeric 1-5 also accepted but discouraged.

name.description: Threshold tier: critical, error, warning, or info. Also used as the routing label - alerts carry threshold_name equal to this value.

op.description: Comparison operator. Canonical literals: above, below, equal, not_equal, above_or_equal, below_or_equal, outside_bounds. Short forms accepted: eq, not_eq, above_or_eq, below_or_eq. Symbolic accepted: >, <, =, !=, >=, <=. Numeric 1-7 also accepted but discouraged.

recoveryTarget.description: Hysteresis - value at which a firing alert is considered resolved. Useful to avoid flapping near the threshold (e.g. target=80 percent, recoveryTarget=75 percent). Use null to use the threshold target itself as the recovery point.

targetUnit.description: Unit of the threshold target value. If different from compositeQuery.unit the backend converts between them during evaluation. Common values: percent, ms, s, bytes, ns.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this alert monitors.

disabled.description: Whether the alert rule is disabled. Defaults to false (enabled).

evalWindow.description: v1 schema only (anomaly_rule). Evaluation window as a Go duration string (e.g. 5m, 15m, 1h, 24h). For threshold/promql rules, use evaluation.spec.evalWindow instead.

evaluation.description: v2alpha1 only. Evaluation configuration. kind=rolling (sliding window) auto-generated with defaults (5m/1m) if omitted; kind=cumulative (daily/monthly reset) for period-total alerts such as daily error counts or Cost Meter spend budgets. Skipped entirely for anomaly_rule which uses top-level evalWindow/frequency instead.

kind.description: Evaluation kind: rolling (sliding lookback window) or cumulative (accumulates from a fixed daily/monthly reset boundary). Cumulative works for any period-total alert (e.g. daily error counts, monthly request budgets); Cost Meter spend budgets are one common use.

spec.description: Evaluation specification. For kind=rolling set evalWindow + frequency; for kind=cumulative set schedule + frequency + timezone.

evalWindow.description: Rolling kind only. Evaluation window as a Go duration string (e.g. 5m, 15m, 30m, 1h, 4h, 24h).

frequency.description: Evaluation frequency as a Go duration string (e.g. 1m, 5m, 15m).

schedule.description: Cumulative kind only. Fixed reset boundary the accumulation window starts from.

hour.description: Hour of the reset boundary (0-23); e.g. 0 for midnight.

minute.description: Minute of the reset boundary (0-59); e.g. 0 for the top of the hour.

type.description: Reset cadence: daily or monthly.

timezone.description: Cumulative kind only. IANA timezone for the schedule boundary (e.g. UTC).

frequency.description: v1 schema only (anomaly_rule). Evaluation frequency as a Go duration string (e.g. 1m, 5m, 3h). For threshold/promql rules, use evaluation.spec.frequency instead.

labels.description: Labels for the alert rule. MUST include severity (one of critical, error, warning, info). When thresholds is used, threshold.name (e.g. critical) acts as the routing tier - set labels.severity to match the highest tier you want this rule to carry. Additional labels like team/service/environment enable routing policies.

notificationSettings.description: v2alpha1 only. Notification settings - controls grouping and re-notification behavior. Auto-generated with defaults if omitted.

groupBy.description: Fields to group alert notifications by (e.g. service.name, k8s.namespace.name). Reduces notification noise by batching alerts with the same group key.

newGroupEvalDelay.description: Grace period (Go duration string, e.g. 2m) during which a newly-appearing label group is excluded from evaluation. Helps avoid flapping when new pods/services come online.

alertStates.description: Alert states that trigger re-notification. Accepted values: firing, nodata. Other values are rejected.

interval.description: Re-notification interval as a Go duration string (e.g. 15m, 30m, 1h, 4h).

usePolicy.description: Routing mode. false (default) = deliver to channels listed in each threshold entry. true = ignore per-threshold channels and route via the org-level notification policy matching on labels.

preferredChannels.description: Notification channel names to send alerts to. Use signoz_list_notification_channels to discover available channel names.

ruleType.description: Evaluation type: threshold_rule (compare against value) or promql_rule (PromQL expression) or anomaly_rule (anomaly detection on metrics).

schemaVersion.description: Schema version. Set to v2alpha1 automatically for threshold_rule/promql_rule. Must be omitted (or empty) for anomaly_rule.

searchContext.description: The user's original question or search text that triggered this tool call. Always include the user's raw query here for better results.

source.description: Source URL for the alert. Set automatically.

version.description: API version. Always v5. Set automatically if omitted.

tools.name: signoz_update_alert

alert.description: Name of the alert rule. Must be unique and descriptive.

alertType.description: Signal type: METRIC_BASED_ALERT or LOGS_BASED_ALERT or TRACES_BASED_ALERT or EXCEPTIONS_BASED_ALERT.

annotations.description: Annotations like description and summary. Supports template variables: {{$value}} for current metric value and {{$threshold}} for the threshold and {{$labels.key}} for label values.

condition.description: Alert condition containing the query and threshold configuration.

absentFor.description: Minutes (equivalent to consecutive evaluation cycles when frequency is 1m) to wait with no data before firing an absent-data alert. Example: absentFor=15 with frequency=1m fires after 15 evaluations return no series.

alertOnAbsent.description: Alert when no data is received within the evaluation window.

algorithm.description: v1 (anomaly_rule) only. Anomaly detection algorithm. Accepted values include standard (z-score based). Used only when ruleType=anomaly_rule.

compositeQuery.description: The composite query defining what data to monitor.

panelType.description: Panel type. Use graph for alerts. Defaults to graph.

queries.description: Array of queries. At least one query is required.

aggregations.description: Aggregation expressions for builder queries. For metrics signal use the object shape: [{metricName: k8s.pod.cpu_request_utilization, timeAggregation: avg, spaceAggregation: max}]. For logs/traces use the expression shape: [{expression: count()}] or [{expression: p99(duration_nano)}].

expression.description: Aggregation expression (logs/traces signal). Examples: count(), avg(duration), p99(duration_nano), count_distinct(user_id), sum(bytes). Do not set metricName/timeAggregation/spaceAggregation when using this shape.

metricName.description: Metric name (metrics signal only). Example: k8s.pod.cpu_request_utilization. Use alongside timeAggregation and spaceAggregation. Do not set expression when using this shape.

spaceAggregation.description: Cross-series space aggregation (metrics signal only). Common values: sum, avg, min, max, count. For histograms use percentiles: p50, p75, p90, p95, p99.

timeAggregation.description: Per-series time aggregation (metrics signal only). Common values: avg, max, min, sum, rate, increase, count, count_distinct, latest. Default by metric type: gauge→avg, cumulative counter→rate, delta counter→sum.

expression.description: Formula expression referencing other query names (e.g. A / B * 100). Used for builder_formula type.

filter.description: Filter expression for builder queries. Example: {expression: service.name = frontend AND http.status_code >= 500}.

expression.description: Filter expression using field operators. Example: service.name = frontend AND http.status_code >= 500. Use empty string for no filter.

functions.description: Post-query functions applied to the series. Required for anomaly_rule: wrap with {name: anomaly, args: [{name: z_score_threshold, value: 2}]}.

args.description: Function arguments. Example for anomaly: [{name: z_score_threshold, value: 2}].

name.description: Argument name (e.g. z_score_threshold).

value.description: Argument value. Can be number, string, or bool depending on the function.

name.description: Function name (e.g. anomaly for ruleType=anomaly_rule).

groupBy.description: Fields to group by. Grouped dimensions appear as labels in alert notifications.

fieldContext.description: Field context: resource for resource attributes or tag for span/log attributes. Required for non-top-level fields.

fieldDataType.description: Data type of the field: string or int64 or float64 or bool.

name.description: Field name to group by (e.g. service.name or http.method or severity_text).

having.description: Having clause to filter aggregation results.

expression.description: Filter expression using field operators. Example: service.name = frontend AND http.status_code >= 500. Use empty string for no filter.

name.description: Query name (e.g. A or B or C). Used as reference in formulas and selectedQueryName.
Field or aggregation expression to order by (e.g. timestamp or count()).

query.description: PromQL or ClickHouse SQL query string. Used when queryType is promql or clickhouse_sql. PromQL with OTel dotted metric names MUST use the Prometheus 3.x UTF-8 quoted-selector form: {"metric.name.with.dots"}. Underscored / __name__ / bare-dotted forms return no data. Read signoz://promql/instructions for the full guide (histogram patterns dotted labels pre-flight checklist).

signal.description: Signal type for builder queries: metrics or logs or traces. Required for builder_query type.

source.description: Data-source filter for metrics builder_query only. Set to meter to alert on Cost Meter (usage/billing) metrics such as signoz.meter.log.size; omit otherwise.

stepInterval.description: Step interval in seconds for time aggregation. Use 60 for metrics alerts.

type.description: Query envelope type. Must match compositeQuery.queryType: builder → builder_query or builder_formula; promql → promql; clickhouse_sql → clickhouse_sql. Also accepted: builder_trace_operator for trace operator queries.

queryType.description: Query type: builder for Query Builder or promql for PromQL or clickhouse_sql for ClickHouse SQL.

unit.description: Unit of the queried data (Y-axis unit). Used for value formatting in alert messages and for unit conversion with targetUnit in thresholds. Common values: percent, ms, s, bytes, ns, reqps, ops.

matchType.description: v1 (anomaly_rule) only. Match type - same accepted values as threshold.matchType (at_least_once, all_the_times, on_average/avg, in_total/sum, last).

op.description: v1 (anomaly_rule) only. Comparison operator applied to the anomaly score - same accepted values as threshold.op (above, below, equal, not_equal, above_or_equal, below_or_equal, outside_bounds).

requireMinPoints.description: Require a minimum number of data points before evaluating the condition.

requiredNumPoints.description: Minimum number of data points required when requireMinPoints is true.

seasonality.description: v1 (anomaly_rule) only. Seasonality pattern for anomaly detection: hourly, daily, or weekly.

selectedQueryName.description: Which query name triggers the alert (e.g. A or B or F1). Required when multiple queries exist. Defaults to the first query name.

target.description: v1 (anomaly_rule) only. Threshold value compared against the anomaly score.

thresholds.description: v2alpha1 only (threshold_rule, promql_rule). Each threshold level (critical, error, warning, info) can route to different notification channels. Required unless alertOnAbsent is true. Omit entirely for anomaly_rule - use condition.op/matchType/target there instead.

kind.description: Threshold kind. Currently only basic is supported.

spec.description: Array of threshold specifications. Each threshold can route to different channels.

channels.description: Notification channel names for this threshold tier. Use signoz_list_notification_channels to discover available names. Ignored when notificationSettings.usePolicy is true.

matchType.description: How to evaluate the threshold. Canonical: at_least_once, all_the_times, on_average, in_total, last. Aliases accepted: avg (=on_average), sum (=in_total). Numeric 1-5 also accepted but discouraged.

name.description: Threshold tier: critical, error, warning, or info. Also used as the routing label - alerts carry threshold_name equal to this value.

op.description: Comparison operator. Canonical literals: above, below, equal, not_equal, above_or_equal, below_or_equal, outside_bounds. Short forms accepted: eq, not_eq, above_or_eq, below_or_eq. Symbolic accepted: >, <, =, !=, >=, <=. Numeric 1-7 also accepted but discouraged.

recoveryTarget.description: Hysteresis - value at which a firing alert is considered resolved. Useful to avoid flapping near the threshold (e.g. target=80 percent, recoveryTarget=75 percent). Use null to use the threshold target itself as the recovery point.

targetUnit.description: Unit of the threshold target value. If different from compositeQuery.unit the backend converts between them during evaluation. Common values: percent, ms, s, bytes, ns.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this alert monitors.

disabled.description: Whether the alert rule is disabled. Defaults to false (enabled).

evalWindow.description: v1 schema only (anomaly_rule). Evaluation window as a Go duration string (e.g. 5m, 15m, 1h, 24h). For threshold/promql rules, use evaluation.spec.evalWindow instead.

evaluation.description: v2alpha1 only. Evaluation configuration. kind=rolling (sliding window) auto-generated with defaults (5m/1m) if omitted; kind=cumulative (daily/monthly reset) for period-total alerts such as daily error counts or Cost Meter spend budgets. Skipped entirely for anomaly_rule which uses top-level evalWindow/frequency instead.

kind.description: Evaluation kind: rolling (sliding lookback window) or cumulative (accumulates from a fixed daily/monthly reset boundary). Cumulative works for any period-total alert (e.g. daily error counts, monthly request budgets); Cost Meter spend budgets are one common use.

spec.description: Evaluation specification. For kind=rolling set evalWindow + frequency; for kind=cumulative set schedule + frequency + timezone.

evalWindow.description: Rolling kind only. Evaluation window as a Go duration string (e.g. 5m, 15m, 30m, 1h, 4h, 24h).

frequency.description: Evaluation frequency as a Go duration string (e.g. 1m, 5m, 15m).

schedule.description: Cumulative kind only. Fixed reset boundary the accumulation window starts from.

hour.description: Hour of the reset boundary (0-23); e.g. 0 for midnight.

minute.description: Minute of the reset boundary (0-59); e.g. 0 for the top of the hour.

type.description: Reset cadence: daily or monthly.

timezone.description: Cumulative kind only. IANA timezone for the schedule boundary (e.g. UTC).

frequency.description: v1 schema only (anomaly_rule). Evaluation frequency as a Go duration string (e.g. 1m, 5m, 3h). For threshold/promql rules, use evaluation.spec.frequency instead.

id.description: UUIDv7 of the alert rule to update (required). Obtain it from signoz_list_alert_rules or signoz_get_alert.

labels.description: Labels for the alert rule. MUST include severity (one of critical, error, warning, info). When thresholds is used, threshold.name (e.g. critical) acts as the routing tier - set labels.severity to match the highest tier you want this rule to carry. Additional labels like team/service/environment enable routing policies.

notificationSettings.description: v2alpha1 only. Notification settings - controls grouping and re-notification behavior. Auto-generated with defaults if omitted.

groupBy.description: Fields to group alert notifications by (e.g. service.name, k8s.namespace.name). Reduces notification noise by batching alerts with the same group key.

newGroupEvalDelay.description: Grace period (Go duration string, e.g. 2m) during which a newly-appearing label group is excluded from evaluation. Helps avoid flapping when new pods/services come online.

alertStates.description: Alert states that trigger re-notification. Accepted values: firing, nodata. Other values are rejected.

interval.description: Re-notification interval as a Go duration string (e.g. 15m, 30m, 1h, 4h).

usePolicy.description: Routing mode. false (default) = deliver to channels listed in each threshold entry. true = ignore per-threshold channels and route via the org-level notification policy matching on labels.

preferredChannels.description: Notification channel names to send alerts to. Use signoz_list_notification_channels to discover available channel names.

ruleType.description: Evaluation type: threshold_rule (compare against value) or promql_rule (PromQL expression) or anomaly_rule (anomaly detection on metrics).

schemaVersion.description: Schema version. Set to v2alpha1 automatically for threshold_rule/promql_rule. Must be omitted (or empty) for anomaly_rule.

searchContext.description: The user's original question or search text that triggered this tool call. Always include the user's raw query here for better results.

source.description: Source URL for the alert. Set automatically.

version.description: API version. Always v5. Set automatically if omitted.
